content.rendered:
Our own year-end wrap-up, because everyone else is doing theirs…

It’s that time of year when every company on the planet seems to be sharing its 2025 Wrapped. From your favourite streaming service reminding you how many hours you spent listening to 90s throwbacks, to brands celebrating their “top moments”, we thought we’d jump on the bandwagon too.

So, here it is: the Travel Money Top 5 Most-Bought Currencies of 2025. Packed with sunshine, city breaks, long-haul dreams and a little global context sprinkled in for fun.

Let’s dive in.

1. Euro (EUR)

Europe was buzzing in 2025, and British travellers wasted no time making the most of it. With new flight routes, cheaper off-season deals, and a surge in foodie-focused trips to Spain, Italy and Portugal, the euro comfortably held the number-one spot.

Whether it was sipping sangria in Seville or pastries in Paris, the Eurozone continued to charm—and our customers kept topping up their travel wallets accordingly.

2. US Dollar (USD)

The US saw a bumper tourism year thanks to blockbuster events, a strong dollar economy, and the irresistible pull of all things American—from the theme parks of Florida to the bright lights of Vegas.

And despite the USA finding itself regularly in the political headlines this year (when isn’t it?), British travellers didn’t seem put off in the slightest. If anything, curiosity, culture and classic American adventures kept the queues at JFK, LAX and Orlando as busy as ever.

3. Turkish Lira (TRY)

Turkey continued to be the crowd-pleaser of 2025. All-inclusive bargains in Antalya and Marmaris, stunning beach holidays at wallet-friendly prices, and a huge spike in interest for Istanbul’s food and culture scene kept the Turkish lira high in the rankings.

Despite economic ups and downs, holidaymakers embraced Turkey’s value and variety—making TRY one of the most popular currencies again this year.

4. Thai Baht (THB)

Thailand’s allure only grew stronger in 2025. With remote-working visas expanding, cheaper long-haul flights, and the eternal love for island-hopping between Phuket, Koh Samui and Krabi, the Thai baht had a stellar year.

The Land of Smiles delivered everything from jungle adventures to beachfront bliss, and Brits were more than happy to pack their swimsuits and stock up on baht.

5. UAE Dirham (AED)

Dubai and Abu Dhabi continued their rise as luxury-on-a-budget escapes. The UAE drew in travellers with glittering new attractions, ever-popular winter sun, and hotel deals that looked too good to be true.

The UAE dirham rounded out our top five thanks to travellers chasing sunshine, shopping, and skyscraper views.

Looking Back at 2025

From sun-seekers to city explorers, 2025 proved that travel is well and truly thriving. The world kept moving, currencies kept trading, and holiday memories kept being made.

And while digital payments grow every year, one thing remains true: cash is still king and we hope it stays that way.

@graph.description: Travel money news and tips from Travel FX

@graph.name: Lucy Reed
@graph.description: Hi! I’m Lucy, as well as writing blog posts I lead Customer Experience and UX at Travel FX. With over 15 years’ experience in the foreign exchange industry, I specialise in helping UK travellers understand exchange rates, avoid unnecessary fees, and feel confident about their travel money before they fly. Having lived both in the UK and overseas, and travelled extensively over the years, I understand first-hand how important it is to get the details right when you’re planning a trip. From managing your travel budget to choosing the right foreign currency or travel money card, small decisions can make a big difference.
